group_1.textRight: ...And that's how it went.
The riverside concert was suu~uuper exciting~!
Everyone from the office was more suuuu~uuuuper@mad, though...

group_1.textLeft: Performing a new song as you please,@sending it out all over the net,
and on top of that, deciding to declare the title@yourself...

group_1.textRight: Never did I imagine at my age
I would cry my eyes out from being scolded~...

group_1.textLeft: What were you thinking...

group_1.textRight: But I have no regrets!
And I also wanted to give you my thanks, Rita...

group_1.textLeft: Me? What did I do?

group_1.textRight: The title of that song...@"#I'm Like You, I'm With You"
was from a hint you gave
from what you you said on your Dotube livestream~

group_1.textCenter: between myself and those@who speak poorly of the East.

group_1.textLeft: So I did, then...

group_1.textRight: When I was listening to the stream@it didn't quite click with me,
but after the trouble by the river―@That's when I finally got it,
and was hit with inspiration!

group_1.textLeft: And I think that is quite literally plagiarism, but...@Well, it's fine.

group_1.textRight: But, why did you say that on the spot, Rita?

group_1.textLeft: Eh?

group_1.textRight: Something must have made you realize...
[chara:303301:effect_emotion_surprise_0][se:7226_shock]Ahh! Not good! I'm going to be late for class!

group_1.textLeft: ...?@[wait:1.0][chara:301001:lipSynch_1]School is already over, though...?

group_1.textRight: I have to make up for the time I missed during@recording~!

group_1.textLeft: The life of a test-taker is a tough one.

group_1.textRight: Well then, Rita, thank you@so much for everything!
If you'll excuse me~

group_1.textCenter: (Why I said that during the livestream...)

group_1.textLeft: The perfume ad from last month was taken down?!
...We were supposed to start shooting next week...

group_1.textRight: Yes...It wasn't in my power...@I'm terribly sorry...
I was looking forward to becoming your manager,@but this is how it is...
An absolute shame.

group_1.textLeft: Hmph! Well, that's fine.
In the modeling world, it's natural for there@to be competition for work opportunities.
If anything, I am the one who@didn't put forth the effort.
That brand must have found@a very good model, then.

group_1.textRight: That's... The reason is a bit different...

group_1.textLeft: What do you mean?

group_1.textRight: The right values change daily...@They advance, right...
Companies have to protect their image,
so they constantly change to the "current values".

group_1.textLeft: ...What are you telling me?

group_1.textRight: As I was saying, the brand has conformed@to those new values
to appeal by showing they don't distinguish...@between how people look.
Their policy is now to not use@people who look well off.

group_1.textLeft: [chara:301001:effect_emotion_surprise_0][se:7226_shock]...?!

group_1.textRight: So they seem to be applying that policy@immediately with next week's ad...
...They're already on board with the current values.
The appeal of beautiful looks― the yearning to@be the object of desire, to be fawned over―
has now become a bad thing.

group_1.textLeft: What...is this...

group_1.textRight: Of course there are opinions that this is all crazy...!
It's the complete opposite...
But hey, you will be okay!
I ensure that you will not lose your job!
I will always stand by your side!
So trust your mother! Don't give up!

group_1.narration: But not because I lost my advertising work...

group_1.textCenter: ("Wanting to look beautiful is a bad thing" now?)

group_1.narration: I made a wish to Kyubey and got these@beautiful looks I'd always wanted.
Meaning in my heart I assigned good and bad@based on appearances.
Of course, I don't hurt others@because of their looks.
But, this value of [textRed:rejecting]@those who look bad in favor of beauty...
The value of purely desiring to protect oneself,@while unknowingly hurting others...@I started to notice that.

group_1.textCenter: (That's why I was able to say that@during the livestream...)

group_1.textRight: ...Um, what kind of melancholic daydream are you@having, Ria-senpai?

group_1.textLeft: Eh?

group_1.textRight: You promised you'd taste test my newest omelette@today.

group_1.textLeft: Ahh, I'm terribly sorry. I will head there right away.
...Hey, have you ever cursed your cooking skills?

group_1.textRight: Sure have. I'm still pretty inexperienced, after all.
Though it's less cursing and@more wanting to improve.

group_1.textLeft: ...Not in that way.@I mean comparing your own skills...
For example, feeling bad for those who have poor@cooking skills...

group_1.textRight: 'Scuse me?

group_1.textLeft: Blaming your good cooking for hurting people who@aren't good at it...
Or being condemned because of it...

group_1.textRight: I've never given so much as an ounce of thought@about that,
and there's been zero people who've told me that.@Zero.

group_1.textLeft: Is...that so...

group_1.textRight: And as I just said,
while I am pretty good, there's still a@"long way to go."
There's always someone above you.
Such problems belong to the world's greatest chefs...@Unless you're a "god", that is.
It's all nothing but arrogance and hubris, isn't it?
I'm just saying before worrying about being at the@top of mankind, you have to improve yourself first.

group_1.textLeft: I see, it's arrogance, then...
Thank you.

group_1.textRight: Eh, for what?
Have you been scrounging up weird food again?
